days.session: Rest — ankle rehab 2×/day, ice if swollen
Brisk walk 8 km — test: 60+ min pain-free?
Run-walk: 5× (2 min jog / 3 min walk), flat tarmac
Easy run + 4× 20 sec strides (if ankle stable)
Easy run — first light trail, taped
Easy run + dike session A light (8 reps)
Long run at event effort, with vest
Dress rehearsal: long run, full kit + event nutrition
Long run back-to-back at event effort
Travel to Guildford — rest, walk, hydrate
Dike session B — stairs (12 reps) + strength
Dike session B+D — stair emphasis (15 reps) + strength

morning.name: Ankle alphabet
morning.description: seated, trace A–Z with the big toe, injured side. 1 full alphabet.

morning.name: Single-leg balance
morning.description: barefoot, eyes open then closed; progress to cushion, then to cushion + head turns. 3x30 sec each side.

morning.name: Banded eversion
morning.description: loop band around forefoot, press outward against resistance. 3x15 reps.

morning.name: Single-leg heel raises
morning.description: slow up, 3 sec down, on a step once pain-free. 3x10 injured side.
morning.reps: 3 sec down, on a step once pain-free

morning.name: 90/90 hip switches
morning.description: sit on floor, both knees at 90 degrees, rotate side to side. 10 reps each side.

morning.name: Half-kneeling hip flexor stretch
morning.description: rear knee down, squeeze glute, lean forward. 45 sec each side. Add lateral lean away from rear leg for deeper TFL stretch.

morning.name: Open book rotations
morning.description: lie on side, knees stacked, rotate top arm open to floor. 8 reps each side.

morning.name: Cat-cow
morning.description: on all fours, 10 slow reps. Move each vertebra.

morning.name: Thread the needle
morning.description: from all fours, reach one arm under and through. 8 each side.

morning.name: Wall calf stretch
morning.description: 45 sec each side, straight leg then bent knee.
morning.reps: 45 sec each side, straight leg then bent knee

morning.name: Tibialis raises
morning.description: stand on edge of step, raise toes toward shin. 15 reps.

morning.name: World's greatest stretch
morning.description: lunge, plant hand, rotate open, reach overhead. 5 each side.

postrun.name: Couch stretch (rear foot on bench/wall)
postrun.description: 60 sec each side. Best hip flexor opener.

postrun.name: Deep squat hold
postrun.description: 60 sec. Ankles, hips, thoracic spine all at once.

postrun.name: Quad foam roll
postrun.description: 60 sec each leg. Roll from hip to just above knee, pausing on tender spots.

strength.name: Copenhagen adductor plank
strength.purpose: Inner thigh resilience for cambered footpaths

strength.name: Step-downs (slow eccentric, off step/bench)
strength.purpose: Eccentric quad strength for stair descents

strength.name: Single-leg squat to bench
strength.purpose: Quad endurance + balance — protects the ankle

recovery_routine: Within 30 min of finishing: 60–80g carbs + 20g protein
Legs up 20 min, then shower, then short walk before dinner
Next morning: ankle rehab block + 5 min mobility before breakfast

sessions.name: Volume Climbing
sessions.description: Steady effort up, easy jog down. Builds cardiac endurance for the rolling North Downs profile.

sessions.name: Steep Repeats
sessions.description: The Trappenmarathon key session: stair rhythm with strong arm drive. Controlled descent, focus on technique.

sessions.name: Downhill Focus
sessions.description: Easy uphill, progressively faster downhill. Builds eccentric quad strength and ankle stability. Skip until week 6 — grass camber is the re-sprain scenario.

sessions.name: Mix Session
sessions.description: All three variants combined. Grass up + asphalt down, stairs up + grass down, asphalt up + stairs down.

periodization.focus: No dike work — flat, even surfaces only; rehab is the hill session
A light in week 5, then A+B 1x/week; no C before week 6
Session B is the priority, 1–2x/week — Trappenmarathon prep

scenarios.label: Target
scenarios.conditions: Solid prep, ankle managed, walk breaks on schedule

scenarios.label: Realistic
scenarios.conditions: Matches the 21h34 route estimate — extra walking on rough ground

scenarios.label: Conservative
scenarios.conditions: Ankle forces walk-heavy stages — still finishes, just longer days

key_factors: Ankle stability on uneven ground — rehab compliance in weeks 1–6 decides it
Back-to-back recovery routine — eat within 30 min, legs up, sleep
Stage 1 discipline — hardest stage on day 1; going out too fast taxes days 2–4
Stair economy — variant B sessions in weeks 11–12 set up the Trappenmarathon
